originalBehavior: Players move a camera over the crowd, snap photos of notable moments, and feed those moments back into the TV broadcast loop.
localStatus: The original camera, director, and TV runtime remain vendored with no structural rewrite.
notes: The route still depends on the upstream PIXI scene graph and camera callbacks instead of a DOM reimplementation.
Relative asset paths were preserved so the capture loop works under the GitHub Pages subpath.
evidence: Local smoke testing on 2026-03-10 confirmed the stage loads and progresses into the camera-driven play scene.
Scene and game modules still wire photo callbacks through the original director and TV logic.

originalBehavior: Captured moments become TV chyron headlines that escalate the crowd's mood and push later scenes toward panic or violence.
localStatus: Headline selection, chyron text, and escalation logic remain in the upstream scene scripts with an explicit local English text map.
notes: The translation bundle was collapsed into one explicit runtime map so public JS no longer carries creator or supporter text.
The TV and chyron system still uses the original world-state checks and scene callbacks.
evidence: Parity scope for this route depends on headline escalation because it is the entire thesis of the game.
Current source still drives those transitions through the upstream Act I to Act III scene stack.

originalBehavior: Different peep behaviors react to headlines, fear, and violence so the crowd progressively spirals from curiosity into hostility.
localStatus: Peep classes, watcher replacement, panic propagation, and violent escalation remain in the original runtime modules.
notes: The local cleanup avoided changing the escalation rules or the crowd replacement choreography.
Audio and screen-shake effects still come from the original scene and peep modules.
evidence: The recheck plan explicitly called out crowd feedback escalation as a parity target for this port.
The vendored route still escalates through the same peep classes and Act III sequence.

originalBehavior: After the violent spiral, the route shifts into a quiet post-credits peace scene before landing on the final replay screen.
localStatus: The peace scene remains upstream-driven, while the public credits and post-post-credits screens were replaced with neutral local equivalents.
notes: The quiet candlelit scene and photo-triggered transition stay in place because they are part of the original emotional arc.
Creator, supporter, and external social-share surfaces were removed and replaced with a replay-only ending card.
evidence: Local route review preserved the post-escalation calm scene and the replay button target.
The local ending scenes now keep only neutral closeout copy plus replay behavior.

originalBehavior: The published bundle lives as a standalone game shell with minimal outer chrome and no site-wide wrapper.
localStatus: The local shell keeps that lightweight structure while adding canonical metadata and the shared reference footer at the document level only.
notes: Public provenance is now limited to the shared footer reference link to the original live page.
The local shell leaves the canvas runtime untouched instead of trying to inject controls inside the PIXI stage.
evidence: The route now loads under /interactive-explanation/wbwwb/ with a shared footer and local canonical metadata.
Audit coverage passes with the route included in the public-surface sweep.
